tenants.tagline: Real goods for the seasons no one warns you about.
A Southwest Florida yard, handled — weekly maintenance and landscaping.
Every legal advantage the tax code gives you — found, explained, and organized.

recent_audit.detail: Phase 1 run 4/4 — QA'd the 48-entry Loophole Catalog: valid JSON, schema-correct (all required fields, no stray fields), 48 unique ids/no duplicates, every entry carries a real IRS source, professional_needed=true on all 15 high-complexity/high-risk entries (no schema violations), estimated_impact always ranged and 'could'-framed with no guarantee language — NO corrective fixes required. Noted all 48 entries are current_as_of 2025 and keep figures qualitative by design; built a figure-verification list, priority clusters QSBS/Opportunity Zones/gift-estate exemption/401(k) catch-ups/bonus depreciation. Drafted Phase 2 lead magnet lead-magnet-loophole-checklist.md ('The Loophole Checklist', five-persona, plain non-hype voice, educational-not-advice disclaimer). Wrote PHASE-1-PROGRESS.md. Outcome: catalog complete and QA-passed; advisor agent spec-complete but not built. Studio repo untouched; no git push.

Phase 1 run 3/4 — drafted the tax-strategist advisor agent in advisor-agent/. SKILL.md: spec covering purpose, situation inputs, personalized strategy-set outputs, five-persona detection, persona-filter + eligibility-match + ranking retrieval, mandatory educational-not-advice disclaimer, and the professional-escalation path. AGENT-DESIGN.md: system-prompt architecture and catalog-grounding mechanics — whole-catalog in-context retrieval over the 48 sourced entries, explicit out-of-catalog "I do not have a sourced strategy for that" behavior (no guessing), disclaimer enforced by defense in depth, verbatim IRS source citation from each entry. Draft and design only; no production code, studio repo untouched.

Phase 1 run 2/4 — added Loophole Catalog Block B: 26 new sourced entries covering small-business, investors-high-earners, and legal-entities personas (catalog now 48 total). Every entry carries a real IRS citation, current_as_of 2025, ranged "could" impact; dollar limits kept qualitative pending verification against current IRS guidance. professional_needed=true set on all entity/trust/high-complexity strategies.

Phase 1 run 1/4 — built Loophole Catalog Block A: loophole-catalog/catalog.json with 22 sourced entries across individuals-families and freelancers-creators personas. Every entry carries a real IRS citation, current_as_of 2025; dollar limits kept qualitative pending verification against current IRS guidance.
Phase 0 — Life Loophole tenant scaffolded: constitution, ops folder, loophole-catalog schema. Buildout plan at ~/Documents/Life-Loophole-Buildout-Plan.md

opportunities.rationale: This market is growing rapidly and demonstrates demand for both physical tools and digital knowledge. By offering a curated line of aesthetically pleasing yet highly functional foraging tools (physical product) combined with comprehensive, modern digital guides (info-product/newsletter), Day14 can serve a community looking to combine their love for nature with a contemporary lifestyle.

human_todos.title: Get an Anthropic API key — unblocks all agent builds
human_todos.detail: Builds (Kennum and every future business) keep dying on the Gemini 429 quota. The durable fix is the Claude fallback, now wired into the whole bootstrap chain — it just needs a key. Generate one at console.anthropic.com -> Settings -> API Keys (requires enabling API billing). Then paste the key to Claude; Claude will add it to .env.local + Vercel and re-run the Kennum build for you.

steps: Open the Anthropic Console and sign in (or create an account).
Go to Settings -> Billing and enable API billing / add a payment method — API keys do not work without it.
Go to Settings -> API Keys and click Create Key; copy the key (starts with sk-ant-).
Paste the key straight to Claude in chat — do NOT type it into any file yourself.
Claude adds it as ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in .env.local + Vercel and re-runs the Kennum build.

human_todos.title: Add MAILERLITE_API_KEY to .env.local + Vercel
human_todos.detail: Newsletter signup on day14.us and every brand site stays inert until this key is set. Get it from mailerlite.com -> Integrations -> Developers -> API, then paste it to Claude to wire in.

steps: Open MailerLite and sign in (a free account is fine to start).
Go to Integrations -> Developers -> API (Account settings).
Generate a new API token and copy it.
Paste the token straight to Claude in chat — do NOT type it into any file yourself.
Claude adds it as MAILERLITE_API_KEY in .env.local + Vercel so newsletter signup goes live.

human_todos.title: Publish the 10 Hot Flash Co product drafts in Printify
human_todos.detail: Agents generated 10 menopause-humor mug designs and created the product drafts. Open Printify -> Hot Flash Co -> publish them to the Pop-Up Store so they go live.

steps: Open Printify and sign in.
Switch to the Hot Flash Co shop and open My Products.
Find the 10 menopause-humor mug drafts the agents created.
Review each draft, then publish it to the Pop-Up Store so it goes live.
Reply "done 4" to the Telegram bot once all 10 are published.

human_todos.title: Confirm Kennum Lawn Care's real phone, service area + pricing
human_todos.detail: The Kennum site is live with starting-at price estimates and a quote form (no phone listed yet). Send Claude the real business phone number, the exact SWFL towns served, and confirmed pricing so the site shows real numbers instead of estimates. Source of truth: src/app/brands/kennum-lawn-care/theme.ts.

steps: Gather the real Kennum business phone number.
List the exact Southwest Florida towns the business actually serves.
Confirm the real pricing (not estimates) for each service offered.
Send all three to Claude in chat — Claude updates src/app/brands/kennum-lawn-care/theme.ts and redeploys so the site shows real numbers.

human_todos.title: Feed the real estate intake — drop a county property CSV
human_todos.detail: The real estate segment is built and the agents run, but they have no data yet. Download a property-appraiser data export from a county site and drop the CSV into businesses/day14-realty/intake/. The intake agent ingests + evaluates it on the next scout run.

steps: Open a Florida county property-appraiser site and find its data download / export page.
Download the property-roll export as a CSV file.
Move the CSV into ~/Documents/businesses/day14-realty/intake/ on the Mac.
The intake agent ingests and evaluates it automatically on the next scout run.

human_todos.title: Run AlignMD Supabase migration 0011_credentialing.sql
human_todos.detail: Phase 1 credentialing workflow. Adds the credentialing_items table (per-provider packet checklist) plus RLS mirroring 0003/0007. Idempotent and safe to re-run. Until it is applied, the new Credentialing tab shows a clean empty state — the app will not crash.

steps: Open the AlignMD Supabase project → SQL Editor.
Paste the contents of supabase/migrations/0011_credentialing.sql and run it.
Confirm the credentialing_items table and its RLS policies exist.
Reload a provider's Credentialing tab — the packet checklist is now editable.

human_todos.title: Build-check & deploy AlignMD credentialing (Phase 1)
human_todos.detail: Overnight run added the credentialing packet (migration 0011, lib/credentialing.ts, credentialing-actions.ts, credentialing-panel.tsx, and a new Credentialing tab on the provider page). npx tsc --noEmit and npx next lint both pass. The overnight sandbox could not finish npx next build (per-call time limit) or reach Vercel to deploy, so this needs an operator run.

steps: Run the production build locally and confirm it passes clean.
Deploy to production once the build succeeds.
Smoke-test the Credentialing tab on a provider at alignmd.vercel.app.
instructions.code: cd ~/Documents/alignmd && npx next build && npx vercel --prod

human_todos.title: Build-check & deploy AlignMD job-feed v2 (block 2)
human_todos.detail: Overnight block 2 built job-feed v2: staff scanned-jobs view (/jobs/scanned) with a Refresh-now trigger, clinician save/interested jobs, and sharper specialty inference. tsc --noEmit and next lint both pass clean; next build could not finish inside the overnight sandbox's 45s per-command limit, so the build gate was not verified and nothing was deployed. Run the build locally, then deploy.

steps: Pull the latest changes on the AlignMD repo.
Run the full build to confirm it passes.
If the build is clean, deploy to production.
Apply migration 0012_saved_jobs.sql first (see the related to-do) so the saved-jobs feature works.
instructions.code: cd ~/Documents/alignmd && npx next build && npx vercel --prod

human_todos.title: Build-check & deploy AlignMD clinician portal polish (block 3)
human_todos.detail: Overnight block 3 made the clinician portal provider-first: a credentialing-progress widget, a profile-completeness meter with specific nudges, and a matched-jobs preview on the portal home, plus mobile-friendly table wrapping across the portal pages. npx tsc --noEmit and npx next lint both pass clean; npx next build could not finish inside the overnight sandbox's 45s per-command limit, so the build gate was not verified and nothing was deployed. Run the build locally, then deploy. No new migration — the widgets reuse credentialing_items (0011) and external_jobs (0010) and degrade to empty states if those tables are not yet applied.

steps: Pull the latest changes on the AlignMD repo.
Run the full production build to confirm it passes clean.
If the build is clean, deploy to production.
Smoke-test the clinician portal home at alignmd.vercel.app — credentialing progress, profile strength, and matched jobs.
instructions.code: cd ~/Documents/alignmd && npx next build && npx vercel --prod

human_todos.title: Generate brand identity for Hot Flash Co
human_todos.detail: hot-flash-co has no brand-identity.json, so its site falls back to default colors and fonts. Run the brand-identity-generator script in the studio repo.

steps: Open Terminal and change into the studio repo: cd ~/Documents/studio
Run the brand-identity-generator command below.
It writes brand-identity.json for hot-flash-co; confirm there are no errors.
Reload the hot-flash-co site to confirm it now uses real colors and fonts.
instructions.code: cd ~/Documents/studio && node scripts/brand-identity-generator.mjs --slug hot-flash-co --display-name "Hot Flash Co" --niche "Real goods for the seasons no one warns you about."

human_todos.title: Publish blog content for Hot Flash Co
human_todos.detail: No blog drafts exist yet for hot-flash-co, so the blog page is empty. The daily content engine generates them; confirm its LaunchAgent is installed, then review and mark drafts status: live.

steps: Confirm the daily content engine LaunchAgent is installed and running (check launchctl list).
Wait for it to generate blog drafts into businesses/hot-flash-co/blog-drafts/, or trigger it manually.
Review each draft markdown file for quality.
Set the front-matter status field to "live" on the drafts you approve so they appear on the blog page.
instructions.code:
launchctl list | grep day14   # confirm the content-engine LaunchAgent is loaded
ls ~/Documents/businesses/hot-flash-co/blog-drafts/

human_todos.title: Add a property-valuation API key (REALESTATE_API_KEY)
human_todos.detail: Optional but recommended. The evaluation layer already scores deals from county data alone; a licensed API (RentCast is cheapest) adds AVM value + rent estimates for sharper flip/rental scores. Generate a key, then paste it to Claude to add as REALESTATE_API_KEY.

steps: Open RentCast and sign in or create an account (it has the cheapest plan).
Go to the API section and generate an API key.
Paste the key straight to Claude in chat — do NOT type it into any file yourself.
Claude adds it as REALESTATE_API_KEY in .env.local + Vercel; the evaluation layer then adds AVM value + rent estimates.

human_todos.title: County records needed: Collier County, FL
human_todos.detail: Day14 needs the public-records property roll for Collier County, FL. Download the CSV from the county property appraiser / open-data portal and drop it into businesses/day14-realty/intake/ named "collier-county.csv". The scout ingests, scores, and starts monitoring it automatically.

steps: Open the Collier County Property Appraiser site and find its data download / export page (or search "Collier County FL property appraiser data export").
Download the full property-roll export as a CSV.
Rename the file to exactly "collier-county.csv".
Move it into ~/Documents/businesses/day14-realty/intake/ on the Mac.
The scout ingests, scores, and starts monitoring it automatically on the next run.

steps: Open the Lee County Property Appraiser site and find its data download / export page (or search "Lee County FL property appraiser data export").
Download the full property-roll export as a CSV.

human_todos.title: Get a free Adzuna API key — AlignMD job scanning
human_todos.detail: AlignMD now scans live healthcare job postings into the clinician portal. The Remotive source works with no key; Adzuna adds broad US healthcare/nursing coverage. Register a free Adzuna developer account, create an app, and paste the App ID + App Key to Claude to add to the alignmd Vercel project as ADZUNA_APP_ID and ADZUNA_APP_KEY.

steps: Open the Adzuna developer portal and register a free account.
Create an application to get your App ID and App Key.
Paste both values straight to Claude in chat — do not type them into any file yourself.
Claude adds them as ADZUNA_APP_ID and ADZUNA_APP_KEY in the alignmd Vercel project; the job feed then includes Adzuna postings.

human_todos.title: Run AlignMD Supabase migration 0012_saved_jobs.sql
human_todos.detail: Job-feed v2 added the saved_jobs table (clinician 'interested' shortlist). Apply supabase/migrations/0012_saved_jobs.sql in the AlignMD Supabase project. Until it runs, the portal Open-jobs page shows an info banner and the Save toggle is hidden — no crash.

steps: Open the AlignMD project in the Supabase dashboard.
Go to the SQL Editor.
Paste the contents of supabase/migrations/0012_saved_jobs.sql and run it.
The migration is idempotent — safe to re-run if unsure.

human_todos.title: Get a free USAJOBS API key — AlignMD job scanning (optional)
human_todos.detail: Optional extra source for the AlignMD job feed — federal clinical roles (VA hospitals, etc.). Request a free USAJOBS API key, then paste the key plus your registered email to Claude to add as USAJOBS_API_KEY and USAJOBS_EMAIL in the alignmd Vercel project.

steps: Open the USAJOBS developer site and request an API key (free).
Note the email address you registered with — USAJOBS requires it alongside the key.
Paste the key and the email straight to Claude in chat — do not type them into any file yourself.
Claude adds them as USAJOBS_API_KEY and USAJOBS_EMAIL in the alignmd Vercel project.
